role: A client-side URL-query detector that flags when the surface is running in non-persisting verification mode.
capabilities: Default to real-write mode when query param absent
Safely return false when window.location is unavailable
substance_why: Only the detection helper is implemented; the banner, per-click notice, and actual write-suppression logic are described in comments but live elsewhere.
interactions_missing: No actual suppression of decision_take persistence
No persistent 'Verification mode' banner rendered here
No per-click Notice naming the suppressed take
No guard for generic annotation/reaction/favour writes
No mode toggle or explicit opt-in UI
reuse_verdict_why: The detection rule and safety default are correct and well-documented, but the redesign should centralize the guard in the write dispatcher and add a dedicated UI indicator.
decomposition_hints: Keep the pure isVerifyMode() detection in a lib/util
Move banner/notice presentation into a VerifyModeIndicator component consumed by App
Move write-suppression policy into the decision_take dispatcher and annotation hooks
